data.title: Kinder Morgan shuts two Los Angeles fuel pipelines as wildfires rage
data.description:
Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI) said late Thursday it has shut down two of its pipelines, SFPP West and CALNEV, due to power outages linked to the wildfires now raging in southern California.

The company said the pipelines are not directly impacted by the fires, and it expects them to resume service once power has been restored.

There have been no reports of refinery outages in California so far due to the wildfires.

The 515-mile SFFP West pipeline moves fuels from the Los Angeles Basin to Colton, Imperial, California, and Phoenix, Arizona, while the 566-mile CALNEV moves gas, diesel and jet fuel from Colton, California, to terminals in Barstow, California and Las Vegas.

Kinder Morgan (KMI) shares +0.5% in early trading Friday to a 52-week high of $28.82, as the energy infrastructure company continues to capitalize on robust demand in the energy sector.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow fall sharply as jobs report comes in hot
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages tumbled on Friday, as investors digested the hotter than expected jobs report.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.7%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.3%.

U.S. Treasury yields surged on Friday, as investors dumped bonds after the latest jobs report came in hotter than expected and further supported a case for fewer Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.75%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 7 basis point to 4.34%.

U.S. markets were shut on Thursday for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

The next test for markets will now be the U.S. jobs report for December, Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Payrolls came in more than expected in December, pushing down the unemployment rate.

"The payrolls number is derived from a survey that has a 43% response rate, and that poor representation means significant revisions are much more likely. The market is looking for a fairly dull report overall—some moderation in job creation with a stable unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Moreover, the U.S. consumer sentiment nudged down to 73.2 in January's preliminary estimate, versus the 74.9 consensus level according to the University of Michigan's Surveys of Consumers.

data.title: Consumer sentiment slips in January as inflation expectations climb
data.description: U.S. consumer sentiment nudged down to 73.2 in January's preliminary estimate, vs. 74.9 consensus from December's 74.0, according to the University of Michigan's Surveys of Consumers released on Friday. Consumers raised their inflation expectations for both the one- and five-year horizons.

data.title: US10Y flirts with 4.80%, US30Y touches 5.00% after hot December jobs report
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields surged on Friday, as investors dumped bonds after the latest jobs report came in hotter than expected and further supported a case for fewer Federal Reserve interest rate cuts.

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) rose as much as 10 basis points to 4.79%, approaching levels not seen since the end of October 2023. The 30-year yield (US30Y) hit 5.00%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up about 7 basis points.

The US2Y is approaching 4.37% - a noteworthy resistance level as the instrument has pushed into the 4.35%-4.38% range multiple times since November but has not been able to crack above. Any move north of 4.38% would mark the highest level for the US2Y since July 30.

See how yields are trading across the entire curve here on Seeking Alpha’s bond page.

Earlier, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said that nonfarm payrolls climbed by 256K in December, significantly outpacing the forecast of +157K. At the same time the unemployment rate edged down to 4.1% versus the 4.2% consensus marker and 4.2% prior reading.

The data pointed to a labor market that has once again become resilient after showing considerable cooling last year - an indication that an easing Fed does not want to see.

Here are some exchange-traded funds tied to the fixed-income market:

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), and (TIP).

data.title: Mercury General expects wildfire losses to exceed reinsurance retention level
data.description:
Mercury General (NYSE:MCY) estimated that its total losses for the California wildfires will exceed its reinsurance retention level of $150M, it said on Friday.

The company's reinsurance program provides $1.29B of limits on a per occurrence basis after covered catastrophe losses exceed the company's retention of $150M, according to its preliminary assessment of the impact of the wildfires that started on Jan. 7, 2025.

Mercury General's (NYSE:MCY) stock dropped 17% in early regular-hours trading on Friday. That has moderated from the over 30% decline in premarket trading.

Mercury General's (MCY) reinsurance program also provides coverage for assessments from the California FAIR plan, the state-run insurer of last resort. To the extent that losses are reinsured, the reinsurance program calls for reinstatements of limits to cover future events. If the full $1.29B limits are used up, then the total reinstatement premium would be $101M, the company said.

data.title: Why Wynn Resorts' casino purchase in London might have more to do with the UAE
data.description:
Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) made an interesting investment with the acquisition of Crown London (Aspinalls), which is described as a small, members-only casino in the heart of London's upscale Mayfair district that caters to premium and VIP customers.

The company thinks the acquisition of the iconic asset will give it a presence in a global gateway city, as well as creating a conduit for guests to visit other resorts, particularly Wynn Al Marjan Island which is slated to open in the first quarter of 2027 in Ras Al Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates.

Crown London offers 20 gaming tables across various sophisticated salons, as well as an intimate restaurant and lounge for entertaining. The casino will operate under the Crown London name until the transaction closes.

CBRE Equity Research analyst John DeCree sees the deal as a valuable customer acquisition tool for Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN). He noted that Aspinalls probably does not generate much cash flow, but sees the casino pickup as a strategic purchase. "Aspinalls caters to very high-end VIP gaming customers from London but also internationally, including from Asia, India, and the Middle East, making it a very unique customer acquisition tool for Wynn Al Marjan Island," he stated. DeCree highlighted that Aspinalls hosts a regular Bollywood Night, and India is one of the top feeder markets for tourism to the UAE.

"This acquisition gives WYNN a flag in a gateway city that should help build the global database and boost Wynn's brand power in the region ahead of a blockbuster opening in the UAE. Importantly, this is a high-end gaming asset with high-intent customers, making it a more deliberate customer acquisition channel relative to a branded hotel strategy or partnership."

Notably, Wynn Resorts (WYNN) does not have a significant history of making acquisitions. Looking ahead, the casino operator is looking at Las Vegas expansion, a potential New York City casino, and development in Thailand for growth, in addition to the significant plans for the UAE.

Shares of Wynn Resorts (WYNN) broke 1.5% higher in Friday morning trading amid a down day for the broad market.

data.title: Citigroup sees restaurant sector facing a challenging 2025
data.description:
Citigroup has a less-than-rosy outlook for the restaurant industry, as the likelihood of a slowing labor market and as inflation outstrips wage gains to “take a bite” out of industry sales. This landscape will likely favor “experiential” names like The Cheesecake Factory (CAKE), and value names like McDonald's (MCD), and Chipotle (CMG), and those that have controllable same store sales drivers including Darden Restaurants (DRI), and Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG).

Taking a closer look at individual names, Citigroup sees a growing narrative around “weaponizing price” and focusing on the value proposition as a key driver for Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG). Additionally, the company’s Infinite Kitchen model is garnering positive reviews, suggesting the potential for “substantial financial upside” for the company.

“We believe management’s confidence in the financial benefits will start to shift the 2025 conversation around a key bear pushback – affordability as a barrier to greater frequency,” Citigroup analysts said in Friday’s research note, leading to an upgrade in Sweetgreen (SG) to Buy/High Risk from Neutral/High Risk and an 11% hike to its price target to $49.

For McDonald’s (MCD), 2025 will be a year when the company “leans back into its scale advantages” after solving the problem of lost value position that negatively impacted same store sales. By revamping its national value platform with its McValue options coupled with McDonald’s (MCD) ability to reach a wider audience through advertising (a marketing budget that is nearly twice Burger King, Wendy’s, and Jack in the Box), its “firepower” is unmatched by direct rivals. This earns the company an upgrade to Buy from Neutral at Citigroup, with a corresponding 7.4% hike in its price target.

On the other hand, the outlook for the international operating environment is less encouraging and likely to persist with lingering risks of global unit closures, ongoing macro challenges in China, and slowing U.S. wage growth potentially translating into reduced customer visits and increased promotional pricing, all of which resulted in a downgrade in Yum! Brands (YUM) to Neutral from Buy.

“Tariffs and geopolitical tensions will drive incremental global uncertainties and investors will favor what seems to be a more predictable (even if slowing) U.S. backdrop,” Citigroup says, leaving Yum! Brands (YUM) along with Restaurant Brands (QSR) and Domino’s Pizza (DPZ) as the worst positioned in this scenario.

At the open, shares of Sweetgreen (SG) are up by 3.6%, McDonald’s (MCD) unchanged, and Yum! Brands (YUM) lower by 1.5%.

data.title: Micron in focus as Citi sees mixed data points on memory market at CES
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) was in focus on Friday as Citi reiterated its Buy rating and $150 price target on the memory maker, citing several mixed data points from CES.

Micron shares fell 2.3% in early trading.

"It appears demand in the PC food chain is a little sluggish, while cell phone demand appears 'ok,'" Citi analyst Christopher Danely wrote in a note to clients.

"However, [dynamic random access memory] inventory in the PC food chain has declined and is close to optimal levels, while in the cell phone food chain it remains elevated. We continue to believe evidence points to DRAM inventory being normalized by the spring, and we expect DRAM pricing to stop declining in 2Q25."

Delving a bit deeper, Danely said that memory demand from the PC market is "a little sluggish," even if DRAM inventory is "close to optimal levels."

As such, this is a "mild negative" for AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC) and Micron, as the trio respectively gets 28%, 55% and 15% of their sales from the PC industry, even if DRAM inventory in

The "ok" demand in the smartphone supply chain should be a "mild positive" for Qualcomm (QCOM), given that it gets roughly 74% of its sales from the space. However, DRAM inventory in smartphones is a "little more elevated," Danely explained.

"Given normal demand trends, we would expect DRAM inventory in the cell phone supply chain to normalize in the spring," Danely added. "Still expect inventory normalization in 2Q25. We continue to expect DRAM inventory to normalize by the spring, and we expect DRAM pricing to stop declining beginning in 2Q25."

data.title: Samsung Bioepis and Teva enter into a partnership for commercialization of EPYSQLI
data.description:
• Samsung Bioepis and Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) announced that the companies have entered into a license, development and commercialization agreement for EPYSQLI, Samsung Bioepis’ biosimilar to Soliris in the U.S..
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Samsung Bioepis will be responsible for the development, regulatory registration, manufacture and supply of the product, while Teva will be responsible for commercialization of the product in the U.S.
  • The financial terms of the agreement remain confidential.
  • This agreement is a testament to both Samsung Bioepis’ and Teva's strong track record in the field of biosimilars and the potential value that these biosimilars could deliver to patients and healthcare systems.
  • Source: Press Release
